summary: Patrick McKenzie answers detailed followup questions about the SEO strategies behind his Bingo Card Creator business. He explains his freelancer workflow for content creation, his preference for forward-facing analysis over backward-facing metrics, and how he reuses product data across multiple marketing channels. Most notably, he describes a conversion optimization technique where new trial users get pre-initialized accounts based on the landing page that brought them in, yielding a 20% improvement in funnel success.
key_points: Content creation is outsourced to a small team of freelancers (primarily one reliable person), with a streamlined CMS workflow that automates page generation from submitted word lists
Forward-facing analysis (finding new opportunities) is more valuable than backward-facing analysis (measuring past results), and domain-specific analytics beat general tools like Google Analytics
Pre-initializing trial accounts with content matching the user's entry point increases conversion by roughly 20%, unifying marketing and product experience
A single piece of content (a bingo card activity) gets reused across content pages, PPC landing pages, desktop software, web app, and cross-site widgets — data begets data
General-purpose analytics tools like Google Analytics provide precision without actionability; custom domain-specific queries yield better business decisions
money_quotes: I do very little backwards facing analysis ('Did that work?') and concentrate mostly on forward facing analysis ('What opportunities can I exploit now?').

You are roughly 20% more likely to successfully download a set of customized bingo cards if I give you the 'personalized' treatment than you are if I drop you at a blank dashboard and expect you to fight your way through.
I don't think enough software companies unify the marketing and product sides, incidentally. We tend to treat everybody coming in to the top of the funnel as absolutely the same.
We've got data that says they are different — why not use the data to enhance their experience and, not incidentally, improve their propensity to buy the product?
If you respond to an ad for Halloween Bingo Cards, then by jove I'm going to everything short of dropping a pumpkin on your desktop.
key_insight: The highest-leverage marketing optimization isn't more content or better analytics — it's connecting the marketing promise to the product experience so users land in a personalized context that matches why they showed up.
